channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Letter Highlights Voter Behaviour, Political Courage, and Gas Prices in U.S. Politics
item.description: The letters published in The Globe and Mail on April 11 cover a range of topics, with one prominent letter focusing on U.S. politics and the potential political consequences for former President Donal...

item.title: Former Ottawa Councillor Reflects on Overcoming Childhood Trauma in New Memoir
item.description: A former Ottawa city councillor has opened up about their experiences of sexual abuse during childhood and their ongoing journey toward healing in a recently published book. The memoir offers a candid...

item.title: US and Iran delegations meet in Islamabad for first direct talks in 47 years
item.description: United States Vice President JD Vance arrived in Islamabad on Saturday to engage in historic, high-level talks with Iranian officials, marking the first direct US-Iran engagement since 1979. The negot...

item.title: Pakistan’s diplomatic role improves global perception following US-Iran ceasefire
item.description: A recent social listening report by Ipsos indicates a major change in how the world views Pakistan’s diplomatic standing after Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif announced a 14-day ceasefire between the Un...

item.title: Binky Felstead accused of not paying bakers for wedding and birthday cakes despite promised social media exposure
item.description: Binky Felstead, known from the reality TV series Made In Chelsea, has faced criticism from two bakery owners who claim they were asked to make cakes for free in exchange for social media exposure. Res...

item.title: US and Iran Begin High-Stakes Negotiations in Islamabad Over Nuclear and Regional Issues
item.description: US and Iranian delegations have started crucial talks in Islamabad, with Pakistan playing host as a neutral facilitator. The negotiations come amid a fragile two-week ceasefire and aim to find a way f...

item.title: Security Measures Tightened in Islamabad and Rawalpindi Ahead of Iran-US Talks
item.description: In anticipation of sensitive negotiations between Iran and the United States, stringent security protocols have been implemented across Islamabad and Rawalpindi. The move reflects the high-profile nat...

item.title: USPS pauses pension payments to maintain operations and requests postage hike
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service announced it will temporarily suspend its employer contributions to the Federal Employees Retirement System annuities to preserve cash flow and continue operations, including p...

item.title: Survey finds many Australians struggle to afford non-PBS medicines
item.description: A recent survey conducted by the McKell Institute has highlighted the financial pressures faced by Australians prescribed medicines not listed on the Pharmaceutical Benefits Scheme (PBS). Nearly half ...

item.title: How Twin Brothers Are Boosting Nutrition Through Spirulina Farming in Kenya
item.description: The article highlights the inspiring journey of twin brothers from Kenya who turned their small spirulina venture into a major food security initiative. Initially starting with modest ambitions to pro...

item.title: NCBA Bank to Auction 34 Repossessed Vehicles Including 2024 Land Cruiser
item.description: NCBA Bank has announced an auction of 34 repossessed vehicles, ranging from high-end models like the 2024 Toyota Land Cruiser 300 to commercial trucks and second-hand saloon cars. Bidders have until A...

item.title: Louisiana’s Republican Senate Primary Shapes Up With Competing Factions Backing Letlow, Cassidy, and Trump
item.description: The upcoming Louisiana Senate race is shaping into a major political showdown within the Republican Party, featuring key figures aligned with Representative Julia Letlow, Senator Bill Cassidy, and for...

item.title: Gerda Steyn and Arthur Jantjies Take Top Prizes at Two Oceans Marathon
item.description: At the 56km Two Oceans Marathon in Cape Town, Gerda Steyn and Arthur Jantjies emerged victorious in the women's and men's races respectively. Steyn secured her seventh consecutive win, demonstrating h...

item.title: Tunji-Ojo Directs Immigration to Withdraw Passports of Nigerians Who Renounced Citizenship
item.description: The Minister of Interior, Dr. Olubunmi Tunji-Ojo, don issue directive for Nigeria Immigration Service to withdraw and deactivate passports of Nigerians wey don renounce their citizenship but still dey...

item.title: Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei Recovering from War Injuries
item.description: Mojtaba Khamenei, the new Supreme Leader of Iran, is still recovering from serious injuries sustained during an airstrike that killed his father and predecessor, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, on the first d...

item.title: Canberra Raiders narrowly beat South Sydney Rabbitohs 36-34 at Optus Stadium
item.description: In a thrilling NRL clash at Optus Stadium, the Canberra Raiders held on to edge out the South Sydney Rabbitohs 36-34. Canberra surged to a 20-point lead by half-time, thanks to early tries from Hudson...

item.title: Finance Ministers of Pakistan and Saudi Arabia Highlight Bilateral Cooperation and Economic Ties
item.description: Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb bid farewell to his Saudi counterpart, Mohammed bin Abdullah Al-Jadaan, at Islamabad International Airport after what was described as a productive one-day visit to...

item.title: Disinformation Campaign Targets Philippine Media with Marcos Jr. Health Rumors
item.description: According to a researcher, Philippine media is experiencing a targeted disinformation campaign centered around rumors concerning the health of President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. This campaign, along with ...

item.title: Trump's White House Renovations Challenge Historical Republican Architectural Ideals
item.description: A U.S. District Court Judge halted construction on President Donald Trump's proposed $400 million White House ballroom on March 31, 2026, emphasizing that the president is a steward, not owner, of the...

item.title: Family Pays for Missed Pickles on BC Ferries, Sparking Widespread Discussion
item.description: A family travelling on the Queen of Cowichan ferry to Vancouver Island had a small but frustrating experience at the White Spot restaurant onboard. William Fraser, 12, ordered his usual burger with no...
